label: Overtext layer label, e.g. manuscript name, part, and script description
locus: Locus of the overtext layer, e.g. ff. 1r-85v
summary: A summary of the overtext layer. Describes the script, date, language, and content in a brief prose statement.
extent: Extent of the overtext layer, e.g. 85 ff.

writing.locus: Locus where this script is used, e.g. ff. 1r-85v
note: A note about this script, e.g. formal Naskh hand throughout.
A second note about the script to show the note field is repeatable.

writing.locus: Locus where a second script is used, e.g. ff. 1r-5v
note: A note about this second script entry, showing multiple writing instances.

ink.locus: Locus for this ink entry, e.g. ff. 1r-85v
note: A note about the ink, e.g. main text in brown iron gall ink.
A second note about the ink to show repeatability.

ink.locus: Locus for a second ink entry, e.g. ff. 1r-10v
note: A note about decorative ink colors used in headings and illuminations.

layout.locus: Locus for this layout entry, e.g. ff. 1r-70v
layout.dim: Writing area dimensions, e.g. 240 x 160 mm
note: A note about the layout, e.g. ruled in drypoint; text written below top line.
A second layout note to show the note field is repeatable.

layout.locus: Locus for a second layout entry, e.g. ff. 71r-85v
layout.dim: Writing area dimensions for the second layout section
note: A note about the second layout, e.g. layout shifts to two columns in final quires.

para.locus: Locus of the paracontent item, e.g. f. 1r
para.as_written: Paracontent text as written in the original script

assoc_name.as_written: Name as written in the paracontent

assoc_date.value: Date as expressed in the paracontent

note: A note about this paracontent item.
A second note to show the note field is repeatable.

para.locus: Locus of the possession note, e.g. f. 90v
para.as_written: Possession note text as written in the original script
translation: English translation of the possession note
note: A note about this possession note.

assoc_date.value: Origin date as a human-readable string, e.g. 10th c. CE
note: A note about this date, e.g. paleographic dating

assoc_date.value: Acquisition date as a human-readable string, e.g. ca. 1200 CE
note: A note about the acquisition date

assoc_name.as_written: Scribe name as written in the manuscript
note: A note about this scribe, e.g. named in the colophon

assoc_name.value: Free-text name value when not linked to an agent record
note: A note about this patron, e.g. referenced in colophon but name illegible

assoc_place.as_written: Place name as written in the manuscript
note: A note about this place, e.g. named in the colophon

note.value: This is a foliation note. It describes the foliation system used in this layer, e.g. modern pencil foliation in Arabic numerals.
This is a condition note. It describes the physical condition of this layer, e.g. water damage, staining, or losses.
This is a collation note. It describes quire structure, catchwords, or signatures relevant to this layer.
This is a general note. It can contain any additional information not covered by other note types.
This is a second general note to show that multiple notes of the same type are allowed and that the note field is repeatable.

bib.citation: Full bibliographic citation string for a reference work cited at the layer level.
bib.alt_shelf: [Alternate shelfmark from an older catalog, e.g. 652]
note: A note about this bibliographic entry

bib.citation: Full bibliographic citation string. Second bib entry at layer level to show repeatability.

related_mss.label: Label for a disjecta membra relationship, e.g. name of the group of related manuscripts
note: A note about this disjecta membra relationship.
A second note to show repeatability.

mss.label: Label of the first related manuscript
Label of the second related manuscript

related_mss.label: Label for a filiation relationship, e.g. related manuscripts in the same text tradition
note: A note about this filiation relationship.

program.label: Description program name
program.description: Full description of the program under which this layer record was created.

cataloguer.message: Cataloguer message describing what was done in this edit.
Second cataloguer entry to show the field is repeatable.
